title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Inspector Hakast and Colin Lamb discuss the girl who discovered the body and expressed extreme terror. Hakast explains the peculiar layout of Wilbraham Crescent, noting that the street is built back-to-back, requiring a detour through Albany Road to reach the second half of the street. Colin searches for number 61, which Hakast identifies as the home of an architect named Brand. Hakast describes Brand as a dishonest man who uses inferior materials to commit commercial fraud. He notes that Brand recently escaped bankruptcy after his Canadian wife inherited a large sum of money following the deaths of her father and grandfather in an air disaster. Hakast mentions that the domestic tax department previously investigated Brand's sudden wealth but found no wrongdoing.

Hakast and Colin then travel to the Cavendish Agency on Wangfujing Street. After passing a photography studio and coal merchants, they enter the agency and are led to the office of Miss Martin-Dell, a woman in her fifties with reddish-brown hair. While Hakast questions her, Colin observes her collection of autographed photos from various mystery and romance novelists. Hakast asks about Sheila Webb, an employee who is currently out on business. Miss Martin-Dell states that Webb has been with the agency for a year and may be at the Heron Hotel for a five o'clock appointment. She adds that Webb previously worked at a firm in London, possibly a property firm, and that her good references are stored in the agency's safe.
